text:
There once was a very tired rabbit. She had hopped through the fields all day, longingly looking at the sun setting on the distant hill. She dreamed of a cozy little bed tucked away under some velvet sky.

Finally, she found a tiny cottage. It was nestled in a dell with thick white fur on its roof. The rabbit pushed open the door and slumped onto a sofa covered in white velvet. She let out a great sigh.

Gentle twilight painted the room, and she looked around. There was a fireplace with a poker resting beside it. She picked it up and kindled a flame. The warmth filled the room. Dinner smelled wonderful, and she ate heartily.

As she brushed her teeth with a twig, she hummed a soothing tune. Hours passed; dusk turned into night. She fell asleep curled up under the warm ashes.

When she awoke, the moon shone high overhead. She hadn’t moved an inch. Maybe she had drifted off... or
